---
title: "councilR: A Toolkit"
subtitle: "Templates, Colors, Functions, and Database Connections for Metropolitan Council R Users"
author: "Metropolitan Council"
date: "2026-06-30"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: council.scss
    logo: main-logo.png
    # footer: "councilR v0.3.0 | Metropolitan Council"
    slide-number: true
    preview-links: auto
    chalkboard: true
    code-fold: false
    code-line-numbers: false
    highlight-style: github
    transition: slide
    background-transition: fade
    advance: true
    scrollable: true
    code-overflow: wrap
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
---
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(councilR)
library(ggplot2)
```

## What is councilR? {.smaller}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="60%"}
**Purpose & Overview**

- Collection of commonly used templates, color palettes, and functions
- Developed by and for Metropolitan Council staff
- Streamlines database connections and spatial data workflows
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
![](logo.png){width=80%}
:::
:::

```r
remotes::install_github("Metropolitan-Council/councilR")
library(councilR)
```

## Package Maintenance & Documentation {.smaller}

**Resources & Support**

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Documentation**

- Vignettes: Colors, Databases, Spatial Helpers, Credentials
- Function help: `?function_name`
- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/Metropolitan-Council/councilR)
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Quality**

- R-CMD-check workflow
- Comprehensive test suite
- Active maintenance
:::
:::

# Branding & Visualization {background-color="#0054A4"}

## Colors & Themes {.smaller}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

**Brand Colors**

- `colors` - Council Brand Standards palette
- `blue_cascade` - Website menu colors, good for reference blues

**Scale Functions**

```r
# Automatic Council palettes (up to 8 levels)
scale_color_council()
scale_fill_council()
```

**Custom Gradients**

```r
colorRampPalette(
  c("#e0e3e8", colors$playDaBlue)
)
```
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**ggplot2 Themes**

```r
theme_council()       # Standard plots
theme_council_open()  # Scatterplots
theme_council_geo()   # Maps
```

**Plotly**

```r
plot_ly(...) %>%
  council_layout(
    main_title = "Title",
    x_title = "X axis",
    y_title = "Y axis"
  )
```
:::
:::

# Database Connections {background-color="#0054A4"}

## Database Overview {.smaller}

**Seamless Data Access**

1. **FRED** - CD Research data (SQL Server)
2. **FRED-Oracle** - Legacy Oracle database
3. **GISLibrary** - Spatial datasets (ESRI SQL)

::: {.callout-tip}
## Security
Credentials managed via `keyring` package. See `vignette("Credentials")` for setup.
:::



## FRED Database {.smaller}

```{r, eval=FALSE}  
# Create connection
library(councilR)
fred <- fred_connection(db = "CD_RESEARCH_WEB", prod = FALSE)

# List available tables
DBI::dbListTables(fred)

# Import a specific table
gq_data <- import_from_FRED(table_name = "GQ_UNIT")

# Always disconnect!
DBI::dbDisconnect(fred)
```

**Contains:** Regional parks, population estimates, group quarters, and more

## FRED-Oracle Database {.smaller}

**Legacy Data Access**

- `FRED_oracle_connection()` - Connect to Oracle database
- `import_from_FRED_oracle()` - Direct table import
- Requires Oracle drivers and specific credentials

```{r, eval=FALSE}
library(councilR)

# create connection
conn <- fred_oracle_connection()

# pull table using SQL
DBI::dbGetQuery(conn, "SELECT * FROM RESEARCH_WEB.RES_PERMIT_TYPE")

# disconnect
DBI::dbDisconnect(conn)

# import a specific table, with no additional SQL logic
import_from_FRED(table_name = "RESEARCH_WEB.RES_PERMIT_TYPE")
```

**Contains:** Some population estimates, housing data

## GISLibrary Database {.smaller}

**Spatial Data Repository**

```r
library(councilR)
# Connect to GISLibrary
gis <- gis_connection()

DBI::dbGetQuery(gis, "select *, Shape.STAsText() as wkt from GISLibrary.dbo.COUNTIES
                      where CO_NAME = 'ANOKA'") %>%
   st_as_sf(wkt = "wkt", crs = 26915)

# Import spatial tables as sf objects
counties <- import_from_gis(
  query = "GISLibrary.dbo.COUNTIES",
  .quiet = TRUE
)

# Non-spatial tables return data frames
lookup <- import_from_gis(
  query = "GISLibrary.dbo.CountyCTULookupTable",
  geometry = FALSE
)
```

# Spatial Data Tools {background-color="#0054A4"}

## Spatial Helper Functions {.smaller}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**fetch_county_geo()**

- Get 7-county metro geography
- Returns sf object
- Optional progress bar

```r
counties <- fetch_county_geo(
  progress_bar = FALSE
)
```
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**fetch_ctu_geo()**

- Get Cities and Townships (CTU)
- Full metro area coverage
- Ready for immediate mapping

```r
ctus <- fetch_ctu_geo(
  progress_bar = FALSE
)
```
:::
:::

## Import Spatial Data {.smaller}

**import_from_gpkg()**

::: {.callout-warning}
Needs to be retired or upgraded for new Geospatial Commons acces
:::

Download and import from Minnesota Geospatial Commons

```r
# Copy link from Geospatial Commons (GeoPackage .zip format)
parks <- import_from_gpkg("https://gis.data.mn.gov/.../parks.zip")
```



## Quick Mapping Function {.smaller}

**map_council_continuous()**


::: {.columns}

::: {.column}


- Scale bar
- Cardinal direction indicator
- Council theme


:::

::: {.column}


```{r}
#| eval: true

fetch_county_geo() %>%
  map_council_continuous(.fill = ALAND) +
  labs(title = "Land Area by County")
```

:::

:::


# Utilities & Productivity   {background-color="#0054A4"}

## RStudio Code Snippets {.smaller}

**Instant Access to Common Values**

Install once: `snippets_install()`

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Available Snippets:**

- `metro_crs` → `26915`
- `tile_crs` → `4326`
- `metro_centroid` → `c(-93.30375, 44.91831)`
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
- `counties` → Seven-county vector
- `counties_mpo` → All MPO counties
:::
:::


::: {.callout-tip}
## Pro Tip
Type snippet name in RStudio and hit Tab to autocomplete!
:::

## Data Exploration with `look()` {.smaller}

**Random Sampling for data.table**

```r
library(data.table)
library(councilR)

titanic <- fread("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/.../titanic_clean.csv")

# Random home destination
titanic[look(home.dest)]

# Chain multiple filters
titanic[look(fare)][look(embarked)]
```

Perfect for exploring large datasets interactively!

## Minnesota FIPS Codes

**Geographic Reference Data**

`mn_fips_codes` dataset:

- County names and FIPS codes
- Essential for joining Census data
- Metropolitan Council region identified
- Ready-to-use reference table

```r
data(mn_fips_codes)
head(mn_fips_codes)
```

# `import_from_gpkg()` {.smaller}


::: {.column}

- `import_from_gpkg()` allows you to import spatial data from a GeoPackage file hosted on the Minnesota Geospatial Commons by providing a direct URL to the gpkg file. 
- Great for public facing code that needs to access spatial data without requiring users to access GISLibrary or other internal databases.

:::

::: {.column}


```{r warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(councilR)

# import the "TPP2050RegionallySignificantProjectPoints" layer only
import_gpkg("https://resources.gisdata.mn.gov/pub/gdrs/data/pub/us_mn_state_metc/trans_tpp2050/gpkg_trans_tpp2050.zip", 
            layer = "TPP2050RegionallySignificantProjectPoints") %>%
head()
``` 

:::


## `{arcgislayers}` {.smaller}

Use new REST API [API](https://gis.data.mn.gov/pages/rest-services)

Import using new REST API with `arcgislayers` package. 

```{r}
library(arcgislayers)

# list items in TPP2050_Highway feature server
arcgislayers::arc_open("https://arcgis.metc.state.mn.us/data2/rest/services/transportation/TPP2050_Highway/FeatureServer/") %>%
  arcgislayers::list_items()


# import layer from REST API
arcgislayers::arc_open("https://arcgis.metc.state.mn.us/data2/rest/services/transportation/TPP2050_Highway/FeatureServer/2") %>%
  head()
```

# Discussion  {background-color="#0054A4"}

## Questions

- Do you use any of the geospatial helper functions in your work?
- Do you have preferences on how we should update `import_from_gpkg()`?
- If we use `{arcgislayers}` for importing spatial data, should we add a wrapper functions to `councilR`?
